quizQuestion:
J'{___} votre montre d'ici demain.
(I will have repaired your watch by tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: I will have repaired your watch by tomorrow.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: réparer (je) — deadline
explanation: aurai réparé — futur antérieur of réparer (je). Formation: avoir au futur simple (j'aurai) + participe passé (réparé). Expresses an action that will be completed before a specific future moment.

quizQuestion:
Est-ce que tu {___} le rapport avant la réunion ?
(Will you have finished the report before the meeting?)
sentenceTranslation: Will you have finished the report before the meeting?
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: finir (tu)
explanation: auras fini — futur antérieur of finir (tu). Formation: tu auras + participe passé fini. Regular -ir verb: participe passé ends in -i.

quizQuestion:
Quand tu arriveras, il {___} sa décision.
(When you arrive, he will have made his decision.)
sentenceTranslation: When you arrive, he will have made his decision.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: prendre (il) — irregular PP
explanation: aura pris — futur antérieur of prendre (il). Irregular participe passé: pris (not prendu). In temporal clauses with quand, the subordinate clause uses futur antérieur when the action is completed before the main clause.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} tout ce qu'il faut savoir avant votre départ.
(We will have said everything you need to know before your departure.)
sentenceTranslation: We will have said everything you need to know before your departure.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: dire (nous) — irregular PP
explanation: aurons dit — futur antérieur of dire (nous). Irregular participe passé: dit (not disé). Formation: aurons + dit.

quizQuestion:
Lorsque vous {___} vos devoirs, vous pourrez sortir.
(Once you have done your homework, you can go out.)
sentenceTranslation: Once you have done your homework, you can go out.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: faire (vous) — irregular PP + lorsque
explanation: aurez fait — futur antérieur of faire (vous). Irregular participe passé: fait (not faisé). Lorsque (once/when) in a future context triggers futur antérieur in the subordinate clause.

quizQuestion:
Dès qu'ils {___} leur article, la revue pourra le publier.
(As soon as they have written their article, the journal can publish it.)
sentenceTranslation: As soon as they have written their article, the journal can publish it.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: écrire (ils) — irregular PP + dès que
explanation: auront écrit — futur antérieur of écrire (ils). Irregular participe passé: écrit. After dès que referring to a future event, French requires the futur antérieur (not the present perfect as in English).

quizQuestion:
Une fois qu'elle {___} le film, elle comprendra mieux.
(Once she has seen the film, she will understand better.)
sentenceTranslation: Once she has seen the film, she will understand better.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: voir (elle) — irregular PP + une fois que
explanation: aura vu — futur antérieur of voir (elle). Irregular participe passé: vu. Une fois que (once) triggers futur antérieur in the subordinate clause when the main clause is in the future.

quizQuestion:
Après que tu {___} ce livre, tu me donneras ton avis.
(After you have read this book, give me your opinion.)
sentenceTranslation: After you have read this book, give me your opinion.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: lire (tu) — irregular PP + après que
explanation: auras lu — futur antérieur of lire (tu). Irregular participe passé: lu. After après que referring to a future event, the futur antérieur is required in formal French.

quizQuestion:
Nous pourrons répondre lorsque nous {___} toutes les candidatures.
(We can respond once we have received all the applications.)
sentenceTranslation: We can respond once we have received all the applications.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: recevoir (nous) — irregular PP + lorsque
explanation: aurons reçu — futur antérieur of recevoir (nous). Irregular participe passé: reçu (note the cedilla on the c). Lorsque (once/when) triggers futur antérieur when describing a completed future action.

quizQuestion:
Il n'est pas encore là ? Il {___} notre rendez-vous.
(He's not here yet? He must have forgotten our appointment.)
sentenceTranslation: He's not here yet? He must have forgotten our appointment.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur: probabilité/conjecture sur le passé (il)
explanation: aura oublié — futur antérieur used to express probability about a past event. Equivalent to 'he must have forgotten'. This speculative use is a key B2 nuance: reasoning about what has probably already happened.

quizQuestion:
Elle n'a pas répondu à mes messages — elle {___} son téléphone dans le taxi.
(She hasn't answered my messages — she must have left her phone in the taxi.)
sentenceTranslation: She hasn't answered my messages — she must have left her phone in the taxi.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur: probabilité/conjecture sur le passé (elle)
explanation: aura raté — futur antérieur used speculatively to infer a past event from present evidence. Raté is the participe passé of rater (regular -er verb). Equivalent to 'she must have left' or 'she has probably left'.

quizQuestion:
Selon nos sources, le ministre {___} cet après-midi.
(According to our sources, the minister has reportedly resigned this afternoon.)
sentenceTranslation: According to our sources, the minister has reportedly resigned this afternoon.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur: journalistique — hedging/conjecture
explanation: aura démissionné — futur antérieur in journalistic hedging style. Used to report unconfirmed information with detachment, equivalent to 'is said to have resigned' or 'reportedly resigned'. Common in formal news writing at B2+ level.

quizQuestion:
Dès que vous {___} la table, nous pourrons commencer le repas.
(As soon as you have set the table, we can start the meal.)
sentenceTranslation: As soon as you have set the table, we can start the meal.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: mettre (vous) — irregular PP + dès que
explanation: aurez mis — futur antérieur of mettre (vous). Irregular participe passé: mis (not mettu). With dès que in a future context, the subordinate clause requires futur antérieur.

quizQuestion:
Aussitôt qu'ils {___} la situation, ils réagiront.
(As soon as they have understood the situation, they will react.)
sentenceTranslation: As soon as they have understood the situation, they will react.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: comprendre (ils) — irregular PP + aussitôt que
explanation: auront compris — futur antérieur of comprendre (ils). Irregular participe passé: compris. Aussitôt que (as soon as) is another temporal conjunction requiring futur antérieur for a completed future action.

quizQuestion:
J'{___} ce projet avant la fin du mois.
(I will have finished this project before the end of the month.)
sentenceTranslation: I will have finished this project before the end of the month.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: terminer (je) — deadline context
explanation: aurai terminé — futur antérieur of terminer (je). Regular -er verb: participe passé terminé. Expresses an action that will be completed by a specific future deadline.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} avant votre retour.
(I will have left before you return.)
sentenceTranslation: I will have left before you return.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: partir (je) — accord du PP
explanation: serai parti(e) — futur antérieur of partir (je). Formation: être au futur simple (je serai) + participe passé (parti/partie). Partir is an être verb. The participe passé agrees with the subject's gender.

quizQuestion:
Quand tu {___} me voir, je te montrerai mes photos.
(When you have come to see me, I'll show you my photos.)
sentenceTranslation: When you have come to see me, I'll show you my photos.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: venir (tu) — accord du PP + quand
explanation: seras venu(e) — futur antérieur of venir (tu). Venir takes être as auxiliary. Participe passé: venu, with gender agreement. Quand triggers futur antérieur in the subordinate clause when the action precedes the main clause.

quizQuestion:
Dès qu'elle {___} à Paris, elle nous appellera.
(As soon as she has arrived in Paris, she will call us.)
sentenceTranslation: As soon as she has arrived in Paris, she will call us.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: arriver (elle) — accord féminin + dès que
explanation: sera arrivée — futur antérieur of arriver (elle). Arriver takes être. Participe passé arrivé agrees with the feminine subject: arrivée. Dès que triggers futur antérieur for a completed future action.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} avant la nuit.
(We will have returned home before nightfall.)
sentenceTranslation: We will have returned home before nightfall.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: rentrer (nous) — accord pluriel
explanation: serons rentrés — futur antérieur of rentrer (nous). Rentrer takes être. Participe passé rentré with masculine plural agreement: rentrés. A mixed-gender group defaults to masculine plural.

quizQuestion:
Lorsque les feuilles {___}, les températures baisseront.
(When the leaves have fallen, the temperatures will drop.)
sentenceTranslation: When the leaves have fallen, the temperatures will drop.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: tomber (ils) — accord pluriel masculin + lorsque
explanation: seront tombés — futur antérieur of tomber (ils). Tomber takes être. Participe passé tombé with masculine plural agreement: tombés. Lorsque triggers futur antérieur in formal French.

quizQuestion:
Une fois que tu {___} en France, tu comprendras l'importance du fromage.
(Once you have been to France, you'll understand the importance of cheese.)
sentenceTranslation: Once you have been to France, you'll understand the importance of cheese.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: aller (tu) — irregular verb + une fois que
explanation: seras allé(e) — futur antérieur of aller (tu). Aller takes être as auxiliary. Futur simple of être: tu seras. Participe passé: allé/allée with gender agreement. Une fois que triggers futur antérieur.

quizQuestion:
Il {___} sans avoir vu la fin de cette histoire.
(He will have died without having seen the end of this story.)
sentenceTranslation: He will have died without having seen the end of this story.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: mourir (il) — irregular PP
explanation: sera mort — futur antérieur of mourir (il). Mourir takes être. Irregular participe passé: mort (not mouré). Masculine singular agreement: mort.

quizQuestion:
Le bébé {___} avant Noël.
(The baby will have been born before Christmas.)
sentenceTranslation: The baby will have been born before Christmas.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: naître — irregular PP
explanation: sera né — futur antérieur of naître. Naître takes être. Irregular participe passé: né/née with gender agreement. Here bébé is masculine, so: né.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} bien avant ton réveil.
(I will have gotten up well before you wake up.)
sentenceTranslation: I will have gotten up well before you wake up.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur pronominal: se lever (je)
explanation: me serai levé(e) — futur antérieur of the pronominal verb se lever (je). Pronominal verbs always take être. The reflexive pronoun (me) precedes the auxiliary (serai). Participe passé: levé/levée with gender agreement.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} avant que vous n'arriviez.
(She will have gotten dressed before you arrive.)
sentenceTranslation: She will have gotten dressed before you arrive.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur pronominal: s'habiller (elle) — accord féminin
explanation: se sera habillée — futur antérieur of s'habiller (elle). Pronominal verbs take être. The reflexive pronoun (se) precedes the auxiliary (sera). Participe passé habillé agrees with the feminine subject: habillée.

quizQuestion:
Les enfants {___} avant que le film ne commence.
(The children will have gone to bed before the film starts.)
sentenceTranslation: The children will have gone to bed before the film starts.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur pronominal: se coucher (ils) — accord pluriel
explanation: se seront couchés — futur antérieur of se coucher (ils). Pronominal verb: être auxiliary + reflexive pronoun (se) before the auxiliary. Participe passé couché with masculine plural agreement: couchés.

quizQuestion:
Elle {___} bien avant minuit.
(She will have fallen asleep well before midnight.)
sentenceTranslation: She will have fallen asleep well before midnight.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur pronominal: s'endormir (elle) — accord féminin
explanation: se sera endormie — futur antérieur of s'endormir (elle). S'endormir takes être as a pronominal verb. Irregular participe passé: endormi, with feminine agreement: endormie.

quizQuestion:
Quand j'{___}, je sortirai me promener.
(When I have finished, I'll go out for a walk.)
sentenceTranslation: When I have finished, I'll go out for a walk.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: finir (je) — séquence quand + futur simple
explanation: aurai fini — futur antérieur of finir (je). Core use: quand + futur antérieur in the subordinate clause; futur simple in the main clause. The completed action (aurai fini) must happen before the second action (sortirai).

quizQuestion:
Appelle-moi dès que tu {___} à la maison.
(Call me as soon as you've gotten home.)
sentenceTranslation: Call me as soon as you've gotten home.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: rentrer (tu) — dès que + impératif
explanation: seras rentré(e) — futur antérieur of rentrer (tu). Rentrer takes être. With dès que in a future context, the subordinate clause requires futur antérieur even when the main clause is an imperative.

quizQuestion:
Après que nous {___} le café, nous partirons.
(After we have had coffee, we will leave.)
sentenceTranslation: After we have had coffee, we will leave.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: boire (nous) — irregular PP + après que
explanation: aurons bu — futur antérieur of boire (nous). Irregular participe passé: bu (not boivé). In written/formal French, après que requires the futur antérieur when the action precedes a future event.

quizQuestion:
Lorsque les élèves {___}, le professeur rangera la salle.
(Once the students have left, the teacher will tidy the classroom.)
sentenceTranslation: Once the students have left, the teacher will tidy the classroom.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: sortir (ils) — accord pluriel masculin + lorsque
explanation: seront sortis — futur antérieur of sortir (ils), intransitive sense (takes être). Participe passé sorti with masculine plural agreement: sortis. Lorsque triggers futur antérieur for a completed future action.

quizQuestion:
Elle ne répond plus — elle {___} la mauvaise nouvelle.
(She's no longer responding — she must have heard the bad news.)
sentenceTranslation: She's no longer responding — she must have heard the bad news.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur: probabilité/conjecture — apprendre (elle)
explanation: aura appris — futur antérieur of apprendre (elle) used speculatively. Irregular participe passé: appris. Infers a past event from present evidence, equivalent to 'she must have learned/heard'.

quizQuestion:
Appelez-moi dès qu'elle {___} du train.
(Call me as soon as she has gotten off the train.)
sentenceTranslation: Call me as soon as she has gotten off the train.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur être: descendre (elle) — accord féminin + dès que
explanation: sera descendue — futur antérieur of descendre (elle), intransitive sense (takes être). Participe passé descendu with feminine agreement: descendue. Dès que triggers futur antérieur.

quizQuestion:
Il {___} tout l'appartement avant que les propriétaires reviennent.
(He will have painted the whole apartment before the landlords return.)
sentenceTranslation: He will have painted the whole apartment before the landlords return.
grammarPointLabel: futur antérieur avoir: peindre (il) — -indre irregular PP
explanation: aura peint — futur antérieur of peindre (il). Irregular participe passé: peint (not peindé). One of the -indre verb family: peindre/peint, craindre/craint, plaindre/plaint.
